paragraphs: Monks Medical is a modern, patient-centred general practice in Plettenberg Bay led by Dr Kyle Rorke. The practice is built on personalised, thorough medical care that integrates conventional general practice with a collaborative, multidisciplinary healthcare model.

The practice provides all the services expected from a trusted family GP — including acute consultations, chronic disease management, routine medicals, pregnancy care, and childhood vaccination programmes — while also offering an expanded healthcare environment designed to optimise long-term wellbeing.

general.title: Primary Care Services
general.paragraph: We offer primary care services to patients of all ages, including routine check-ups, preventive care, and management of acute and chronic medical conditions. Whether you require a routine health assessment, vaccinations, or treatment for common illnesses such as colds, flu, or infections, our team is here to provide compassionate and expert care.

general.title: Chronic Disease Management
general.paragraph: For patients living with chronic medical conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, asthma, or heart disease, we offer comprehensive management plans tailored to individual needs. By emphasising patient education, lifestyle modifications, and appropriate medical interventions, we aim to optimise disease control and improve quality of life.

general.title: Sports Medicine
general.paragraph: With a shared passion for sports and exercise medicine, we offer specialised services to athletes and fitness enthusiasts. Whether you're dealing with sports injuries, musculoskeletal pain, or seeking guidance on performance optimisation, we provide evidence-based treatment and personalised advice to help you stay active and healthy.

general.title: Paediatric Care
general.paragraph: We provide comprehensive paediatric care for infants, children, and adolescents, focusing on preventive health, developmental milestones, and timely immunisations. Whether your child needs a well-baby check-up, vaccinations, or treatment for common childhood illnesses, our team is dedicated to providing gentle and compassionate care to support their growth and development.

additional.title: Nutritional Counselling
additional.paragraph: We offer nutritional counselling and guidance to help patients achieve their health and wellness goals. Whether you're looking to manage your weight, improve your diet, or address specific nutritional concerns, we provide practical advice and support to help you make sustainable lifestyle changes.

additional.title: Mental Health Support
additional.paragraph: We understand the importance of mental health in overall well-being, and we offer compassionate support and evidence-based interventions for patients dealing with stress, anxiety, depression, or other mental health concerns. Through counselling, medication management, and referral to mental health specialists, we are committed to helping you achieve emotional wellness.

additional.title: Men’s Health
additional.paragraph: Men's health is an important aspect of our practice, and we offer services tailored to address the unique health concerns of male patients. From prostate screenings to testosterone replacement therapy, we provide personalized care to support men in maintaining their health and vitality at every stage of life.

additional.title: Unique Services
additional.paragraph: At Monks Medical, we offer a few different services that set us apart. We have a point-of-care blood machine capable of conducting CRP, influenza, and COVID swabs in real-time tests, enabling quick and accurate diagnosis. Additionally, our team is certified to perform point-of-care ultrasound examinations using a Butterfly iQ handheld ultrasound machine, enhancing our diagnostic capabilities and enabling immediate assessment and management of various conditions.

contact.address: Unit 5, Monk’s View Building, 4 Church St, Plettenberg Bay, 6600

faqs.question: Are peptides safe?
faqs.answer: Safety depends on the specific peptide, dosing, and patient suitability. Careful assessment and monitoring are essential.

faqs.question: Are peptides approved medications?
faqs.answer: Some peptides are approved for specific indications, while others are used in evolving areas of medical research. This is discussed transparently during consultation.

faqs.question: Do I need monitoring?
faqs.answer: Yes. Follow-up and clinical review form part of any peptide protocol.

peptides.clinicalNote: Peptide therapy is prescribed under medical supervision and only where clinically appropriate. Treatments are personalised and not universally suitable.

faqs.question: Is IV therapy safe?
faqs.answer: When prescribed appropriately and administered by trained medical professionals, IV therapy is generally well tolerated. Suitability is assessed prior to treatment.

faqs.question: Do I need blood tests first?
faqs.answer: In some cases, laboratory testing may be recommended to guide formulation or ensure safety.

faqs.question: How often can I receive IV therapy?
faqs.answer: Frequency depends on clinical indication and individual response. This is discussed during consultation.

ivNutrientTherapy.clinicalNote: IV nutrient therapy at Monks Medical is delivered within a medical setting and is evidence-informed. It is not intended to diagnose, treat or replace essential medical therapy where indicated.

paragraphs: Monks Medical is a collaborative healthcare practice offering comprehensive general medical services alongside advanced preventative and performance-based therapies.

The practice provides acute care, chronic disease management, pregnancy monitoring, childhood vaccinations, diagnostic ultrasound services, injury rehabilitation and optimisation programmes within a single coordinated environment.

By integrating physiotherapy, ultrasound diagnostics, nursing services and advanced therapeutic modalities, Monks Medical delivers continuity of care while maintaining the standards of a trusted family general practice.

Our mission is to provide personalised, evidence-based care that supports patients at every stage of life — from routine consultations to performance optimisation and long-term preventative health strategies.

team.name: Dr. Kyle Rorke
bio: Dr Kyle Rorke is a general practitioner with a strong commitment to personalised and preventative medicine. He leads Monks Medical with the vision of providing comprehensive, coordinated healthcare within a modern, collaborative clinical environment.

While maintaining the full scope of traditional GP services, Dr Rorke has developed particular expertise in metabolic health, performance optimisation and preventative care. His approach is grounded in clinical medicine while recognising the importance of lifestyle, movement, recovery and long-term disease prevention. He works closely with an integrated team of physiotherapists, a sonographer and a professional nurse to ensure patients receive coordinated, comprehensive care tailored to their individual needs.

Dr Rorke believes that medicine should extend beyond treating illness. His focus is on empowering patients with knowledge, implementing structured health strategies, and supporting sustainable long-term wellbeing.

team.name: Sr. Heilet Van Endt
bio: I am a registered nurse and midwife with a BCur degree, specialising in midwifery, infection control, and hospital management. I run my own practice where I offer comprehensive care for pregnancies, newborns, contraception, and routine women's health services such as Pap smears. I also provide well-baby clinic services, including growth monitoring and paediatric vaccinations, supporting parents through their child's early developmental stages with evidence-based, compassionate care. My focus is on creating a safe, professional, and welcoming environment for every patient.

Beyond my professional role, I am a wife and a mother of two, which gives me a deep appreciation for the realities of family life and parenthood. I enjoy running, reading, and playing classical music on the flute and piano — interests that reflect balance, discipline, and calm. These values shape the holistic and caring approach I bring to my practice and to the families I serve.

team.name: Andrew Jansen Van Rensburg
bio: Bsc Physiotherapy (Hons), Internationally Certified Competitive Triathlon Coach (ITU), Pilates Conditioning (Pretoria).

Andrew was the consultant Physiotherapist for the South African National Triathlon Team from 2008 to 2012, and consults for other professional sporting teams in a rehabilitation and performance enhancement role. He is internationally published on articles regarding core strength and overuse injuries. He has been a Clinical Pilates instructor since 2001 and has modified his approach tfo great effect in the rehabilitation and performance fields.

He has been instrumental in advancing the Clinical Pilates field by using Real Time Ultrasound to assess the efficacy of Transversus Abdominis and Multifidus contractions. The modification of core strength programs by making the programs more functional, and clinically objectively measurable has shown a significant improvement in the long-term rehabilitation rates of his patients.

Andrew is an ITU (International Triathlon Union) Level 2 certified competitive Triathlon Coach and Running Coach, coaching and guiding athletes to all distances of races from Sprint distance Triathlons to Ironman, and from 10km Road running races up to Comrades Marathon. A particular niche of Andrews training is Ultra Distance Trail and road running, having an acute understanding of the intricacies needed to work on in order to succeed in these tough events.

Andrew was the head of the Triathlon South Africa Medical Commission from 2006 to 2016, and ran Triathlon/Duathlon clinics for underprivileged children affected by HIV/Aids at Noahs Ark in Diepsloot, Soweto.

As an athlete Andrew is a sub 3hr Marathon Runner, Ultra Distance Trail runner, Mountain biker and Adventure Racer. He has completed 4 Full Ironman and 20+ Half Ironman races and back to back Comrades Marathons. Currently Andrew competes in Ultra Distance Trail running with top ten finishes in all 8 of South Africa's 100 Mile trail runs, including winning the Cederberg Traverse 100 Miler. In October 2023, Andrew Competed in the World Championships for Adventure Racing where he completed the full course in 7 days with his team, overcoming many adverse conditions along the way.
Active and Balanced lifestyles are what Andrew promotes, and strives to get each individual to achieve their goals and maintain their bodies to be able to achieve what they set out to do.

team.name: Sally Morris
bio: My name is Sally Morris and I was born in Ireland. I went to the UK when I was 18 to study Diagnostic Radiography. I qualified and worked at St Peters hospital in Chertsey, Surrey for approximately 7 years. During that period of time I qualified as a sonographer and worked there for 2 years. I then worked in Cork, Ireland for a period of 6 months.

I came to South Africa in 2004 and worked as a Radiographer/Sonographer at Scribante and Partners in Knysna. For the last ten years I have worked at the Knysna Provincial hospital 3 days a week. Approximatley 18 months ago I joined Monks medical and enjoy working with the community of Plett.

team.name: Jana Van Zyl
bio: Jana graduated from the University of the Western Cape with a Bachelor of Science in Physiotherapy in 2019. She then went on to complete her Community Service year in 2020 at Tygerberg Hospital, where she broadened her knowledge with orthopaedic conditions and post operative care.

At the beginning of 2021, she started working at Marulize Swart Physiotherapists at Louis Leipoldt MediClinic where she mastered new skills and gained valuable experience, especially in treating pediatric conditions and orthopaedics. Continued Musculoskeletal techniques and interest management of pain bolster her skill set. Between spending time with her husband and chasing around after their busy toddler, she has completed the following post graduate courses in Basi Pilates Mat Teacher Training Course and Optimal Dry Needling Solutions.
